answer:
In Allama Iqbal's Urdu poetry in *Bang-e-Dara*, the term *jalwa*—rendered in English translations as "splendor," "splendor manifest," or similar—functions poetically in a manner evocative of Sufi mysticism, denoting the radiant self-disclosure or epiphanic appearance (*tajalli*-like) of divine beauty (*husn*) or reality within the phenomenal world, nature, human forms, or the seeker's heart. It often contrasts partial, veiled revelations (as in the Mosaic theophany on Mount Sinai/Toor) with fuller unveilings accessible to the spiritually perceptive, emphasizing how such manifestations ignite longing, self-realization, and transcendence beyond the material.[^1][^2][^3]

This usage aligns with classical Sufi terminology (e.g., as in Ibn ʿArabī or Rumi, whom Iqbal draws upon), where *jalwa* signifies God's beauty manifesting in creation to draw the soul toward unity, though Iqbal adapts it to his philosophy of dynamic *khudī* (selfhood) and Islamic renaissance. A dedicated poem, "Jalwa-e-Husn" ("The Splendor of Beauty"), exemplifies this: the *jalwa* of beauty stirs restless desire (*tmanna be-taab*), renders the transient world eternal, purifies perception, and subordinates reason to passion, questioning if perfect divine Beauty inheres in creation.[^4]

The corpus does not contain Iqbal's prose works (e.g., *Asrar-i Khudī* or *Reconstruction*), so no explicit definitional treatise appears; its role emerges through recurrent poetic imagery rather than systematic exposition.[^5]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, ch. 25 "Himalaya" (translation), [paras. p-748be3–p-44a04a](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/25-himalaya/translation/#p-748be3).
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, ch. 4 "Jinhein main dhoondta tha..." (translation), [para. p-b6feb6](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/04-jinhein-main-dhoondta-tha-aasmanon-mein-zameenon-m/translation/#p-b6feb6).
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, ch. 14 "Aaftab" (translation), [paras. p-b3de7e–p-abf68a](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-b3de7e).
[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-e-Dara Part 2*, ch. 15 "Jalwa-e-Husn" (translation), [full chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/15-jalwa-e-husn/translation/).
[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-e-Dara Part 1*, ch. 11 "Sakhtiyan karta hoon..." (transliteration), [para. with *jalwa pairaai*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/11-sakhtiyan-karta-hoon-dil-par-ghair-se-ghafil-hoon-/transliteration/#p-a5b391).

args.query: "self manifestation" OR theophany OR splendour OR "divine manifestation" Iqbal
tajalli OR theophany OR "divine manifestation" OR "self-manifestation" Iqbal
"Mount Sinai" OR Toor OR Sinai Iqbal
